name: I waited 3 months to report my shoulder strain from work in West Valley City, can I still get comp?
description: Yes in Utah up to 180 days, but report ASAP to avoid denial risks.[1]

mainEntity.name: I waited 3 months to report my shoulder strain from work in West Valley City, can I still get comp?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes in Utah up to 180 days, but report ASAP to avoid denial risks.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Utah allows 180 days to report without barring your claim, so yes if within that window.[1][2] Delays can lead to insurer doubts or denials, so promptness helps.[1] Key factors: Document why delayed (e.g., symptoms worsened); provide strong evidence like medical records. Insurer has 21-45 days to decide.[2] Practical guidance: Report now in writing; see doctor immediately and link to work. Appeal denials quickly via Labor Commission. Early reporting strengthens validity.[1]
